DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF LAUREL MOUSSEAU, AND LAUREL MOUSSEAU'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges the elements of professional negligence. Additionally, if there are any sufficient allegations to entitle plaintiff to relief, other allegations cannot be challenged by general demurrer. (Kong v. City of Hawaiian Garden Redevelop. Agency (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th 1028, 1046.) Liberally construing the complaint, the allegations do not establish that all of Plaintiff's potential causes of action were barred by the statute of limitations at the time that Plaintiff hired Defendant. For example, Plaintiff alleges that he wanted Defendant to bring a breach of contract cause of action against the union. (Complaint, par. 8.) The statute of limitations for breach of a written contract is four years. The complaint alleges that Plaintiff hired Defendant within four years of his termination (Complaint, pars. 8 and 14.). Order signed in open court. JJudge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
